revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 粉河産土神社 |所在地= [[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]]粉河2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |緯度度 = |経度度 = |地図国コード = JP |祭神= 丹生津比賣命、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] |創建= [[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]） |社格= |札所等= |本殿= [[春日造]] |例祭= 7月最終の土曜日・日曜日 |神事= 粉河祭り |画像= |地図= }} '''粉河産土神社'''（こかわうぶすなじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]]粉河にある[[神社]]。[[粉河寺]]の境内にある。地元では別称として「たのもしの宮」とも呼ばれる。
== 歴史 == [[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）に[[紀伊国]]の猟師・大伴孔子古が[[粉河寺]]を創建し、次いでその子の大伴船主が[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）に鎌垣庄内各村の氏神を勧請して粉河寺の鎮守社として社を建立した、それが当社の始まりであるという。
[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]にあい、粉河寺とともに全焼している。
粉河寺の本堂のすぐ奥にある鎮守社として、[[江戸時代]]には[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川氏|徳川家]]の崇敬を受けていたが、[[明治]]時代の[[神仏分離]]によって粉河寺から独立している。
[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）、旧長田村松井の丹生神社を合祀、同年粉河町東毛の大神社も合祀している。[[1919年]]（大正8年）には粉河町粉河天福神社も合祀している。
当社の祭りである粉河祭りは紀州三大祭の一つである。
[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、裏山より経塚が出土している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 第一殿：丹生津比賣命、第二殿：[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] * 配祀神 - 大伴孔子古命、大伴船主命
== 境内== * 本殿第一殿（丹生大明神社、紀の川市指定[[有形文化財]]） - [[元禄]]年間から[[宝永]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1711年]]）再建。 * 本殿第二殿（若一王子社、紀の川市指定有形文化財） - 元禄年間から宝永年間（1688年 - 1711年）再建。 * 拝殿 * 儀式殿 * 大神社 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に現在地に移築。 * 須佐神社 * 社務所 * おんどり石 * [[熊野神社]] * 吉野神社 * 天福神社（紀の川市指定有形文化財） - [[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）建立。[[1919年]]（大正8年）に現在地に移築。 * 五社神社（[[北野天満宮|北野神社]]、[[多賀神社]]、[[日吉神社]]、[[一言主]]神社、楠神社） * [[護国神社]] * [[白山神社]] * 粉河[[稲荷神社]] * 神輿庫
== 文化財 == === 和歌山県指定無形民俗文化財 === * 粉河祭り渡御式（粉河祭保存会）
=== 紀の川市指定有形文化財 === * 粉河産土神社本殿 2棟 * 天福社本殿
== 祭事 == * 粉河祭り
== 交通アクセス == * [[和歌山線]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]）[[粉河駅]]下車徒歩10分 * [[阪和自動車道|阪和道]]の[[和歌山インターチェンジ|和歌山IC]]から国道24号を東へ、粉河交差点を左折、1.1kmで大門前
== 参考文献 == * 粉河産土神社由来
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010/ 粉河産土神社]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こかわうふすなしんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:紀の川市の神社]] [[Category:和歌山県の歴史]] [[Category:和歌山県の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 粉河産土神社 |所在地= [[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]][[粉河町|粉河]]2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |緯度度 = |経度度 = |地図国コード = JP |祭神= 丹生津比賣命、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] |創建= [[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]） |社格= |札所等= |本殿= [[春日造]] |例祭= 7月最終の土曜日・日曜日 |神事= 粉河祭り |画像= |地図= }} '''粉河産土神社'''（こかわうぶすなじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]][[粉河町|粉河]]にある[[神社]]。[[粉河寺]]の境内にある。地元では別称として「たのもしの宮」とも呼ばれる。
== 歴史 == [[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）に[[紀伊国]]の猟師・大伴孔子古が[[粉河寺]]を創建し、次いでその子の大伴船主が[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）に鎌垣庄内各村の氏神を勧請して粉河寺の鎮守社として社を建立した、それが当社の始まりであるという。
[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]にあい、粉河寺とともに全焼している。
粉河寺の本堂のすぐ奥にある鎮守社として、[[江戸時代]]には[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川氏|徳川家]]の崇敬を受けていたが、[[明治]]時代の[[神仏分離]]によって粉河寺から独立している。
[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）、旧長田村松井の丹生神社を合祀、同年粉河町東毛の大神社も合祀している。[[1919年]]（大正8年）には粉河町粉河天福神社も合祀している。
当社の祭りである粉河祭りは紀州三大祭の一つである。
[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、裏山より経塚が出土している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 第一殿：丹生津比賣命、第二殿：[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] * 配祀神 - 大伴孔子古命、大伴船主命
== 境内== * 本殿第一殿（丹生大明神社、紀の川市指定[[有形文化財]]） - [[元禄]]年間から[[宝永]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1711年]]）再建。 * 本殿第二殿（若一王子社、紀の川市指定有形文化財） - 元禄年間から宝永年間（1688年 - 1711年）再建。 * 拝殿 * 儀式殿 * 大神社 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に現在地に移築。 * 須佐神社 * 社務所 * おんどり石 * [[熊野神社]] * 吉野神社 * 天福神社（紀の川市指定有形文化財） - [[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）建立。[[1919年]]（大正8年）に現在地に移築。 * 五社神社（[[北野天満宮|北野神社]]、[[多賀神社]]、[[日吉神社]]、[[一言主]]神社、楠神社） * [[護国神社]] * [[白山神社]] * 粉河[[稲荷神社]] * 神輿庫
== 文化財 == === 和歌山県指定無形民俗文化財 === * 粉河祭り渡御式（粉河祭保存会）
=== 紀の川市指定有形文化財 === * 粉河産土神社本殿 2棟 * 天福社本殿
== 祭事 == * 粉河祭り
== 交通アクセス == * [[和歌山線]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]）[[粉河駅]]下車徒歩15分 * [[阪和自動車道|阪和道]]の[[和歌山インターチェンジ|和歌山IC]]から国道24号を東へ、粉河交差点を左折、1.1kmで粉河寺大門前
== 参考文献 == * 粉河産土神社由来
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010/ 粉河産土神社]

{{神社 |名称= 粉河産土神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=紀の川市}} |所在地= [[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]][[粉河町|粉河]]2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |緯度度 =34 |経度度 =135 |地図国コード = JP |祭神= 丹生津比賣命、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] |創建= [[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]） |社格= |札所等= |本殿= [[春日造]] |例祭= 7月最終の土曜日・日曜日 |神事= 粉河祭り |地図= |緯度分=16|緯度秒=53.1|経度分=24|経度秒=20.2}} '''粉河産土神社'''（こかわうぶすなじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]][[粉河町|粉河]]にある[[神社]]。[[粉河寺]]の境内にある。地元では別称として「たのもしの宮」とも呼ばれる。
== 歴史 == [[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）に[[紀伊国]]の猟師・大伴孔子古が[[粉河寺]]を創建し、次いでその子の大伴船主が[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）に鎌垣庄内各村の氏神を勧請して粉河寺の鎮守社として社を建立した、それが当社の始まりであるという。
[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]にあい、粉河寺とともに全焼している。
粉河寺の本堂のすぐ奥にある鎮守社として、[[江戸時代]]には[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川氏|徳川家]]の崇敬を受けていたが、[[明治]]時代の[[神仏分離]]によって粉河寺から独立している。
[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）、旧長田村松井の丹生神社を合祀、同年粉河町東毛の大神社も合祀している。[[1919年]]（大正8年）には粉河町粉河天福神社も合祀している。
当社の祭りである粉河祭りは紀州三大祭の一つである。
[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、裏山より経塚が出土している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 第一殿：丹生津比賣命、第二殿：[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] * 配祀神 - 大伴孔子古命、大伴船主命
== 境内== * 本殿第一殿（丹生大明神社、紀の川市指定[[有形文化財]]） - [[元禄]]年間から[[宝永]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1711年]]）再建。 * 本殿第二殿（若一王子社、紀の川市指定有形文化財） - 元禄年間から宝永年間（1688年 - 1711年）再建。 * 拝殿 * 儀式殿 * 大神社 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に現在地に移築。 * 須佐神社 * 社務所 * おんどり石 * [[熊野神社]] * 吉野神社 * 天福神社（紀の川市指定有形文化財） - [[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）建立。[[1919年]]（大正8年）に現在地に移築。 * 五社神社（[[北野天満宮|北野神社]]、[[多賀神社]]、[[日吉神社]]、[[一言主]]神社、楠神社） * [[護国神社]] * [[白山神社]] * 粉河[[稲荷神社]] * 神輿庫
== 文化財 == === 和歌山県指定無形民俗文化財 === * 粉河祭り渡御式（粉河祭保存会）
=== 紀の川市指定有形文化財 === * 粉河産土神社本殿 2棟 * 天福社本殿
== 祭事 == * 粉河祭り
== 交通アクセス == * [[和歌山線]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]）[[粉河駅]]下車徒歩約20分 * [[和歌山バス那賀]]紀伊粉河線「粉河寺」停留所下車徒歩約5分 * [[京奈和自動車道|京奈和道]]の[[紀の川東インターチェンジ|紀の川東IC]]から[[和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|県道122号]]を西へ、[[和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|県道7号]]と合流する交差点を左折、約500mで粉河寺大門前
== 参考文献 == * 粉河産土神社由来
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010/ 粉河産土神社]

{{神社 |名称= 粉河産土神社 |画像= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|粉河産土神社本殿]] |所在地= [[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]][[粉河町|粉河]]2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |緯度度 =34 |経度度 =135 |地図国コード = JP |祭神= 丹生津比賣命、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] |創建= [[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]） |社格= |札所等= |本殿= [[春日造]] |例祭= 7月最終の土曜日・日曜日 |神事= 粉河祭り |地図= |緯度分=16|緯度秒=53.1|経度分=24|経度秒=20.2}} '''粉河産土神社'''（こかわうぶすなじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[紀の川市]][[粉河町|粉河]]にある[[神社]]。[[粉河寺]]の境内にある。地元では別称として「たのもしの宮」とも呼ばれる。
== 歴史 == [[宝亀]]元年（[[770年]]）に[[紀伊国]]の猟師・大伴孔子古が[[粉河寺]]を創建し、次いでその子の大伴船主が[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）に鎌垣庄内各村の氏神を勧請して粉河寺の鎮守社として社を建立した、それが当社の始まりであるという。
[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉|羽柴秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]にあい、粉河寺とともに全焼している。
粉河寺の本堂のすぐ奥にある鎮守社として、[[江戸時代]]には[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川氏|徳川家]]の崇敬を受けていたが、[[明治]]時代の[[神仏分離]]によって粉河寺から独立している。
[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）、旧長田村松井の丹生神社を合祀、同年粉河町東毛の大神社も合祀している。[[1919年]]（大正8年）には粉河町粉河天福神社も合祀している。
当社の祭りである粉河祭りは紀州三大祭の一つである。
[[1959年]]（[[昭和]]34年）、裏山より経塚が出土している。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - 第一殿：丹生津比賣命、第二殿：[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]] * 配祀神 - 大伴孔子古命、大伴船主命
== 境内== * 本殿第一殿（丹生大明神社、紀の川市指定[[有形文化財]]） - [[元禄]]年間から[[宝永]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1711年]]）再建。 * 本殿第二殿（若一王子社、紀の川市指定有形文化財） - 元禄年間から宝永年間（1688年 - 1711年）再建。 * 拝殿 * 儀式殿 * 社務所 * おんどり石 * 神輿庫
=== 摂・末社 === * 大神社 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に現在地に移築。 * 須佐神社 * [[熊野神社]] * 吉野神社 * 天福神社（紀の川市指定有形文化財） - [[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）建立。[[1919年]]（大正8年）に現在地に移築。 * 五社神社（[[北野天満宮|北野神社]]、[[多賀神社]]、[[日吉神社]]、[[一言主]]神社、楠神社） * [[護国神社]] * [[白山神社]] * 粉河[[稲荷神社]]
== 文化財 == === 和歌山県指定無形民俗文化財 === * 粉河祭り渡御式（粉河祭保存会）
=== 紀の川市指定有形文化財 === * 粉河産土神社本殿 2棟 * 天福社本殿
== 祭事 == * 粉河祭り
== 交通アクセス == * [[和歌山線]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]）[[粉河駅]]下車徒歩約20分 * [[和歌山バス那賀]]紀伊粉河線「粉河寺」停留所下車徒歩約5分 * [[京奈和自動車道|京奈和道]]の[[紀の川東インターチェンジ|紀の川東IC]]から[[和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|県道122号]]を西へ、[[和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|県道7号]]と合流する交差点を左折、約500mで粉河寺大門前
== 参考文献 == * 粉河産土神社由来
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 粉河産土神社]

'''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine'''
[[ja:%E7%B2%89%E6%B2%B3%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]]

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

revision.text:
{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.

As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.

=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. {{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto

== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. revision.text:
{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

[[Category:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]] [[Category:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]]

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.

== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. {{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine

=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.

[[Category:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]] [[Category:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{Shinto |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine

[[Category:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]] [[Category:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:okawa Ubusuna Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] [[Category:New]]

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |Image= [[File:粉河産土神社.jpg|250px|Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Main Hall]] |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2}} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kokawa Town|ja|粉河町}}, {{ill|Kinokawa City|ja|紀の川市}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|ja|紀伊国}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins

In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Amenooshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳命}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–17qq). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–17qq). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|ja|粉河駅}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|ja|和歌山線}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyōnaiwa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
