revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|qid=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|qq=|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|qq=|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|qq=|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|qq=|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kokawa Station|Kokawa Station]] on the [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|qq=|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Kyōnaiwa Expressway]], head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|qq=|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|qq=|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|qq=|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|qq=|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|qq=|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|qq=|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kokawa Station|Kokawa Station]] on the [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|qq=|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Kyōnaiwa Expressway]], head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|qq=|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|qq=|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kokawa Station|Kokawa Station]] on the [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Kyōnaiwa Expressway]], head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kokawa Station|Kokawa Station]] on the [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Kyōnaiwa Expressway]], head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.

== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kokawa Station|Kokawa Station]] on the [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Kyōnaiwa Expressway]], head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival

Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.

[[ja:粉河産土神社]] [[ja:%E7%B2%89%E6%B2%B3%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]]

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q106852508}} [[Category:Buildings in Kinokawa, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kinokawa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kokawa, Wakayama|Kokawa Town]], [[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]]. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: [[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-oshihomimi]] * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]]) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kokawa Station|Kokawa Station]] on the [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Kyōnaiwa Expressway]], head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:粉河産土神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852508]] Wikidata name: Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}, {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|en|Kokawa Station|WD=Q3467694|lt=Kokawa Station}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Kyōnaiwa Expressway}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}, {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|en|Kokawa Station|WD=Q3467694|lt=Kokawa Station}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Kyōnaiwa Expressway}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town|1=Kokawa, Wakayama}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|WD=Q697002|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town|1=Kokawa, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|en|Kokawa Station|WD=Q3467694|lt=Kokawa Station|1=Kokawa Station}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604461}} on the {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Kyōnaiwa Expressway|1=Keinawa Expressway}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
== External Links == * [https://ubusuna.org/ Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Official Website * [https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3010 Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building |Name= Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine |image= File:粉河産土神社.jpg |Location= {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|qid=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|qid=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town|1=Kokawa, Wakayama}}, 2788 {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point}} |Latitude degrees=34 |Longitude degrees=135 |Map code= JP |Deities= Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} |Foundation= Enryaku 2 (783) |Rank= |Pilgrimage status= |Main Hall= Kasuga-zukuri |Annual Festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |Rituals= Kokawa Festival |Map= |Latitude minutes=16|Latitude seconds=53.1|Longitude minutes=24|Longitude seconds=20.2|prefecture= {{ill|Kinokawa|qid=Q697002|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.281351|N|135.405669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine''' (粉河産土神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokawa, Wakayama|en|Kokawa, Wakayama|qid=Q6426573|lt=Kokawa Town|1=Kokawa, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|qid=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is situated within the grounds of {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5358314}} and is locally referred to by its alternate name, "Tanomoshi no Miya" (頼母子之宮). Its formal name includes the unique character 𡈽 (土 with a comma above).
== History == In Hōki 1 (770), a hunter from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}, Ōtomo no Kujiko, founded {{ill|Kokawa Temple|ja|粉河寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokawa Temple|1=Kokawa-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5358314}}. Later, his son, Ōtomo no Funamori, established the shrine in Enryaku 2 (783) by enshrining the tutelary deities of the surrounding Kamagaki Shōnai villages to serve as the guardian shrine of Kokawa Temple. This marked the shrine's origin.
In Tenshō 13 (1585), the shrine, along with Kokawa Temple, was destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}} led by Toyotomi Hideyoshi.
As the guardian shrine situated immediately behind the main hall of Kokawa Temple, the shrine was revered by the Tokugawa family, lords of the Kii Domain, during the Edo period. However, it became independent of the temple during the Meiji period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} (separation of Shinto and Buddhism).
In 1912 (Taishō 1), the shrine absorbed the {{ill|Niu Shrine|ja|丹生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niu Shrine|1=Nifuno Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368604}} from Matsui in the former Nagata Village and the Ōkami Shrine from Higashi-ke, Kokawa Town. Later, in 1919 (Taishō 8), it also absorbed the Tenpuku Shrine of Kokawa Town.
The Kokawa Festival, held by the shrine, is one of the three major festivals in the Kishū region.
In 1959 (Shōwa 34), a relic mound was excavated from the mountain behind the shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities - First Hall: Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto, Second Hall: {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} * Subordinate Deities - Ōtomo no Kujiko no Mikoto, Ōtomo no Funamori no Mikoto
== Grounds == * First Main Hall (Niu Daimyōjin Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Second Main Hall (Wakamiya Ōji Shrine, Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Rebuilt between the Genroku and Hōei eras (1688–1711). * Worship Hall * Ritual Hall * Shrine Office * Ondori Stone * Portable Shrine Storage
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * Ōkami Shrine - Relocated to its current location in 1912 (Taishō 1). * Susano Shrine * Kumano Shrine * Yoshino Shrine * Tenpuku Shrine (Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Property) - Built in Kyōhō 12 (1727), relocated to its current location in 1919 (Taishō 8). * Gosha Shrine (Shrines to Kitano, Taga, Hiyoshi, Ichigonushi, and Kusunoki) * Gokoku Shrine * Hakusan Shrine * Kokawa Inari Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Wakayama Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Kokawa Festival Procession Ceremony (Kokawa Festival Preservation Society)
=== Kinokawa City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Two Main Halls of Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine * Main Hall of Tenpuku Shrine
== Annual Events == * Kokawa Festival
== Transportation Access == * About a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kokawa Station|en|Kokawa Station|qid=Q3467694|lt=Kokawa Station|1=Kokawa Station}} on the {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|qid=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} (JR West). * About a 5-minute walk from "Kokawadera" bus stop on the {{ill|Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja|和歌山バス那賀 紀伊粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Bus Naka Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * From the {{ill|Kinokawa East Interchange|ja|紀の川東インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa East Interchange|1=Kinokawa-higashi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604461}} on the {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|qid=Q11374262|lt=Kyōnaiwa Expressway|1=Keinawa Expressway}}, head west on {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|ja|和歌山県道122号西川原粉河線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 122|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 122|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417512}}; turn left at the intersection with {{ill|Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|ja|和歌山県道7号粉河加太線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefectural Route 7|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road Route 7|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417664}}. Kokawa Temple is located about 500 meters from the main gate.
== References == * Kokawa Ubusuna Shrine Origins
